description: Diario de Tratamento — area para pacientes registrarem uso de cannabis medicinal com modelo antes/depois de sintomas, favoritos para quick-log, timeline e dashboard de insights

userStories.title: Prisma models for diary
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the database models to persist diary entries, symptom logs, effect logs, and favorites.
acceptanceCriteria: Add DiaryEntry model to Prisma schema with fields: id (uuid), userId (relation to User), date (DateTime), time (String), productId (optional relation to Product), customProductName (optional String), administrationMethod (String — oil/flower/vape/edible/topical/capsule/other), doseAmount (Decimal 10,2), doseUnit (String — drops/ml/mg/g/puffs/units), notes (optional String), isFavorite (Boolean default false), createdAt, updatedAt

Add DiarySymptomLog model: id, diaryEntryId (relation to DiaryEntry), symptomKey (String), customSymptomName (optional String), severityBefore (String — none/mild/moderate/severe), severityAfter (optional String — same enum)
Add DiaryEffectLog model: id, diaryEntryId (relation to DiaryEntry), effectKey (String), isPositive (Boolean), customEffectName (optional String)

Add DiaryFavorite model: id, userId (relation to User), name (String), productId (optional relation to Product), customProductName (optional String), administrationMethod (String), doseAmount (Decimal 10,2), doseUnit (String), symptomKeys (String[] array), createdAt
Add cascade delete on DiaryEntry for DiarySymptomLog and DiaryEffectLog
Add index on DiaryEntry(userId, date)
Run npx prisma db push successfully
Run pnpm prisma:generate successfully
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Shared enums and predefined lists for diary
userStories.description: As a developer, I need shared constants for administration methods, dose units, severity levels, predefined symptoms, and predefined effects.
acceptanceCriteria: Create file packages/shared/src/diary.ts (or add to existing shared types) with: AdministrationMethod enum (oil, flower, vape, edible, topical, capsule, other), DoseUnit enum (drops, ml, mg, g, puffs, units), SymptomSeverity enum (none, mild, moderate, severe)
Export PREDEFINED_SYMPTOMS array with keys: pain, anxiety, insomnia, nausea, inflammation, fatigue, depression, appetite_loss, headache, muscle_spasm, stress, ptsd
Export PREDEFINED_EFFECTS_POSITIVE array: relaxed, pain_relief, sleepy, calm, focused, euphoric, hungry, creative, energized
Export PREDEFINED_EFFECTS_NEGATIVE array: dry_mouth, dizzy, paranoia, anxious, headache, nausea, drowsy, red_eyes

Add PT-BR labels for all enums and predefined lists to apps/web/src/constants/labels.ts following the existing pattern (ADMINISTRATION_METHOD_LABELS, DOSE_UNIT_LABELS, SYMPTOM_SEVERITY_LABELS, SYMPTOM_LABELS, EFFECT_LABELS)
Export the new types from the shared package index
Typecheck passes in all packages

userStories.title: Diary domain entities and repository interface
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the domain entities and abstract repository for the diary module following the existing DDD pattern.
acceptanceCriteria: Create DiaryEntry entity in src/domain/diary/enterprise/entities/diary-entry.ts extending Entity with props matching Prisma model, following the same pattern as existing entities (User, OnboardingSession)
Create DiarySymptomLog value object or entity in src/domain/diary/enterprise/entities/diary-symptom-log.ts
Create DiaryEffectLog value object or entity in src/domain/diary/enterprise/entities/diary-effect-log.ts
Create DiaryFavorite entity in src/domain/diary/enterprise/entities/diary-favorite.ts

Create abstract DiaryEntriesRepository in src/domain/diary/application/repositories/diary-entries-repository.ts with methods: create, findById, findManyByUserId (paginated with filters), update, delete, countByUserId
Create abstract DiaryFavoritesRepository with methods: create, findById, findManyByUserId, delete
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: CreateDiaryEntry use case
userStories.description: As a patient, I want to create a diary entry with product, dose, symptoms (with severity), and effects.
acceptanceCriteria: Create CreateDiaryEntry use case in src/domain/diary/application/use-cases/ that receives userId, date, time, productId (optional), customProductName (optional), administrationMethod, doseAmount, doseUnit, notes (optional), symptoms array (symptomKey + severityBefore), effects array (effectKey + isPositive)
Returns Either<Error, { entry: DiaryEntry }> following the project Either pattern
Validates that at least product or customProductName is provided
Create in-memory DiaryEntriesRepository in test/repositories/
Create makeDiaryEntry factory in test/factories/
Unit tests covering: successful creation, creation with custom product, creation without symptoms, validation errors
pnpm test passes

userStories.title: UpdateDiaryEntry use case (including symptom re-evaluation)
userStories.description: As a patient, I want to update a diary entry and re-evaluate symptom severity after use.
acceptanceCriteria: Create UpdateDiaryEntry use case that receives entryId, userId, and partial update fields including symptom severityAfter updates
Returns Either<NotFoundError | NotAllowedError, { entry: DiaryEntry }>
Validates that entry belongs to the requesting user
Allows updating severityAfter on existing DiarySymptomLog records
Unit tests covering: successful update, update severityAfter, entry not found, wrong user
pnpm test passes

userStories.title: DeleteDiaryEntry and GetDiaryEntry use cases
userStories.description: As a patient, I want to view and delete my diary entries.
acceptanceCriteria: Create DeleteDiaryEntry use case: receives entryId + userId, validates ownership, deletes entry (cascade deletes symptoms and effects)
Create GetDiaryEntry use case: receives entryId + userId, returns entry with symptoms and effects populated
Both return Either with appropriate errors (NotFound, NotAllowed)
Unit tests for both use cases
pnpm test passes

userStories.title: ListDiaryEntries use case with filters and pagination
userStories.description: As a patient, I want to list my diary entries with filters by date range, product, method, and symptom.
acceptanceCriteria: Create ListDiaryEntries use case: receives userId, page, perPage (default 20), dateFrom, dateTo, productId, administrationMethod, symptomKey (all filters optional)
Returns Either<Error, { entries: DiaryEntry[], total: number }>
Entries ordered by date desc, time desc
Each entry includes symptoms and effects populated
Unit tests covering: list with no filters, list with date range, list with symptom filter, pagination, empty results
pnpm test passes

userStories.title: Diary favorites use cases (CRUD + log from favorite)
userStories.description: As a patient, I want to save favorite combinations and create entries from them for quick logging.
acceptanceCriteria: Create CreateDiaryFavorite use case: receives userId, name, productId/customProductName, administrationMethod, doseAmount, doseUnit, symptomKeys array
Create ListDiaryFavorites use case: receives userId, returns favorites list
Create DeleteDiaryFavorite use case: receives favoriteId + userId, validates ownership
Create CreateEntryFromFavorite use case: receives favoriteId + userId + date + time, creates DiaryEntry pre-filled from favorite data with symptoms pre-selected (severityBefore defaults to none)
Create in-memory DiaryFavoritesRepository in test/repositories/
Create makeDiaryFavorite factory in test/factories/
Unit tests for all four use cases
pnpm test passes

userStories.title: GetDiarySummary use case
userStories.description: As a patient, I want to see a summary of my treatment diary for a given period.
acceptanceCriteria: Create GetDiarySummary use case: receives userId, dateFrom (default 30 days ago), dateTo (default now)

Returns: totalEntries, mostFrequentSymptoms (top 5 with count), mostUsedProduct (name + count), averageSeverityBefore vs averageSeverityAfter per symptom (delta), methodDistribution (map of method to count)
Unit tests covering: summary with data, summary with no entries (returns zeros), summary with date range filter
pnpm test passes

userStories.title: GetSymptomTrend use case
userStories.description: As a patient, I want to see how a specific symptom evolves over time.
acceptanceCriteria: Create GetSymptomTrend use case: receives userId, symptomKey, dateFrom, dateTo
Returns array of data points: { date, avgSeverityBefore (number 0-3 mapping none=0 mild=1 moderate=2 severe=3), avgSeverityAfter (number or null), entryCount }
Group by day, ordered by date asc
Unit tests covering: trend with multiple days, trend with gaps, symptom not found returns empty array
pnpm test passes

userStories.title: Prisma repository implementations and mappers
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Prisma implementations of the diary repositories with proper mappers.
acceptanceCriteria: Create PrismaDiaryEntriesRepository in src/infra/database/prisma/repositories/ implementing DiaryEntriesRepository
Create PrismaDiaryFavoritesRepository implementing DiaryFavoritesRepository
Create DiaryEntryMapper with toDomain() and toPrismaCreate() methods, handling nested symptom logs and effect logs
Create DiaryFavoriteMapper with toDomain() and toPrismaCreate()
Include symptom and effect logs in findById and findManyByUserId queries (Prisma include)
Implement filters in findManyByUserId: dateFrom/dateTo (where date gte/lte), productId, administrationMethod, symptomKey (where symptoms.some.symptomKey equals)
Register repositories in http.module.ts DI (abstract class -> Prisma implementation)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Diary entry controllers (POST, GET list, GET detail, PATCH, DELETE)
userStories.description: As a frontend developer, I need the HTTP endpoints for diary entry CRUD operations.
acceptanceCriteria: Create POST /diary controller: @UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard), receives body with entry data + symptoms + effects, calls CreateDiaryEntry use case, returns 201
Create GET /diary controller: @UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard), query params for page, perPage, dateFrom, dateTo, productId, administrationMethod, symptomKey, calls ListDiaryEntries
Create GET /diary/:id controller: @UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard), calls GetDiaryEntry
Create PATCH /diary/:id controller: @UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard), calls UpdateDiaryEntry (supports severityAfter updates)
Create DELETE /diary/:id controller: @UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard), calls DeleteDiaryEntry
Create DTOs with class-validator decorators for all endpoints
Register controllers and use cases in http.module.ts
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Diary favorites and insights controllers
userStories.description: As a frontend developer, I need the HTTP endpoints for favorites, summary, and symptom trends.
acceptanceCriteria: Create POST /diary/favorites controller: @UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard), calls CreateDiaryFavorite
Create GET /diary/favorites controller: @UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard), calls ListDiaryFavorites
Create DELETE /diary/favorites/:id controller: @UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard), calls DeleteDiaryFavorite
Create POST /diary/favorites/:id/log controller: @UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard), calls CreateEntryFromFavorite
Create GET /diary/summary controller: @UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard), query params dateFrom/dateTo, calls GetDiarySummary
Create GET /diary/symptoms/:key/trend controller: @UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard), query params dateFrom/dateTo, calls GetSymptomTrend
Create DTOs with class-validator for all endpoints
Register in http.module.ts
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: E2E tests for diary API
userStories.description: As a developer, I need E2E tests to verify the diary endpoints work end-to-end with a real database.
acceptanceCriteria: Create diary.e2e-spec.ts in test/e2e/
Test flow: create entry with symptoms and effects -> list entries -> get entry detail -> update entry with severityAfter -> verify delta -> delete entry -> verify deleted
Test favorites flow: create favorite -> list favorites -> log from favorite -> verify entry created
Test summary endpoint returns correct aggregated data
Test auth: verify all endpoints return 401 without JWT
Test ownership: verify user cannot access/modify another user's entries
pnpm test:e2e passes

userStories.title: React Query hooks for diary API
userStories.description: As a frontend developer, I need hooks to consume all diary API endpoints.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/hooks/use-diary.ts with all hooks
useDiaryEntries(filters) — GET /diary with query params, returns paginated data
useDiaryEntry(id) — GET /diary/:id
useCreateDiaryEntry() — POST /diary mutation, invalidates diary list query
useUpdateDiaryEntry() — PATCH /diary/:id mutation, invalidates list and detail queries
useDeleteDiaryEntry() — DELETE /diary/:id mutation, invalidates list query
useDiaryFavorites() — GET /diary/favorites
useCreateDiaryFavorite() — POST /diary/favorites mutation
useDeleteDiaryFavorite() — DELETE /diary/favorites/:id mutation
useLogFromFavorite() — POST /diary/favorites/:id/log mutation, invalidates diary list
useDiarySummary(dateFrom, dateTo) — GET /diary/summary
useSymptomTrend(key, dateFrom, dateTo) — GET /diary/symptoms/:key/trend
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Diary route, lazy loading, and navigation links
userStories.description: As a patient, I need to access the diary page from the navbar and dashboard.
acceptanceCriteria: Add /diario route to App.tsx with React.lazy import, wrapped in ProtectedRoute
Add 'Diario' link to the header/navbar, visible only when user is authenticated (follow existing pattern for authenticated links)
Create placeholder DiaryPage component in src/pages/diary.tsx with page title 'Diario de Tratamento'
Typecheck passes
pnpm build passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Diary reusable UI components (SeveritySelector, SymptomChip, EffectChip, MethodSelector)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the reusable UI components for the diary forms and timeline.
acceptanceCriteria: Create SeveritySelector component: 4 circles (gray=none, yellow=mild, orange=moderate, red=severe) with PT-BR labels, click to select, shows current selection, accepts onChange callback
Create SymptomChip component: chip showing symptom name (from SYMPTOM_LABELS) + SeveritySelector inline, support for custom symptom name
Create EffectChip component: chip with green background (positive) or red background (negative), showing effect name from EFFECT_LABELS, toggleable

Create MethodSelector component: horizontal chips with inline SVG icons (droplet for oil, flower for flower, wind for vape, cookie for edible, hand for topical, pill for capsule) — no emojis, strokeWidth 1.5
All components follow CannHub design system v2 (brand-green palette, DM Sans, rounded corners)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: New diary entry form — product, method, and dose section
userStories.description: As a patient, I want to open a form to log a new diary entry with product selection, method, and dose.
acceptanceCriteria: Add 'Novo registro' button to DiaryPage header that opens a modal/drawer

Modal has sections: 'Quando' with date picker (default today) and time input (default now), 'O que' with product dropdown (fetches products from linked associations via existing useAssociationProducts hooks + 'Outro' option with text input for custom product), 'Metodo' using MethodSelector component, 'Quanto' with numeric input + DoseUnit selector dropdown
Form state managed with controlled components
Responsive: modal on desktop, bottom-sheet drawer on mobile
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: New diary entry form — symptoms, effects, notes, and submit
userStories.description: As a patient, I want to add symptoms (with severity), effects, and notes to my diary entry and save it.
acceptanceCriteria: Add 'Como estou antes' section: grid of SymptomChip components for predefined symptoms, each with SeveritySelector. Button '+ Adicionar sintoma' opens text input for custom symptom

Add 'Efeitos sentidos' section: grid of EffectChip components split into 'Positivos' and 'Negativos' groups, toggleable. Optional section with note that user can fill later. Button '+ Outro' for custom effects
Add 'Notas' section: textarea with placeholder 'Como foi a experiencia?'
Add 'Salvar como favorito' toggle checkbox at bottom
Submit button calls useCreateDiaryEntry, closes modal, shows success feedback, invalidates list
Validation: date + product (or custom name) + dose are required, show inline errors
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Diary timeline — entry cards grouped by day
userStories.description: As a patient, I want to see my diary history as a timeline grouped by day.
acceptanceCriteria: Create DiaryEntryCard component showing: time, method icon (SVG), product name, dose with unit, symptom badges with delta indicator (green arrow down = improvement, red arrow up = worsened, gray dash = no change), truncated notes preview
Main section of /diario page shows entries grouped by day with date headers (e.g., 'Hoje, 06 Abr', 'Ontem, 05 Abr', '04 Abr 2026')
Uses useDiaryEntries hook with pagination
Empty state: SVG illustration + 'Comece registrando seu primeiro uso' text + CTA button that opens new entry form
Load more button or infinite scroll at bottom
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Diary timeline — filters and entry detail/edit
userStories.description: As a patient, I want to filter my timeline and view/edit entry details.
acceptanceCriteria: Add filter bar above timeline: period chips (7d, 30d, 90d, custom date range), method dropdown, product dropdown, symptom dropdown
Filters update URL query params and refetch entries via useDiaryEntries
Click on entry card expands inline or opens detail view showing all fields
Detail view has 'Editar' button that makes fields editable, save calls useUpdateDiaryEntry
Detail view has 'Excluir' button with confirmation dialog, calls useDeleteDiaryEntry
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Symptom re-evaluation flow
userStories.description: As a patient, I want to re-evaluate my symptoms after the product takes effect.
acceptanceCriteria: Entries with symptoms missing severityAfter show amber badge 'Pendente re-avaliacao' on the DiaryEntryCard
Badge has button 'Como estou agora?' that opens mini-modal showing the same symptoms from the entry with SeveritySelector for severityAfter
Save calls useUpdateDiaryEntry with severityAfter values
After re-evaluation, DiaryEntryCard updates to show delta indicators (e.g., 'Dor: moderada → leve' with green arrow)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Quick-log bar with favorites
userStories.description: As a patient who uses the same product daily, I want to re-log with 1-2 taps using saved favorites.
acceptanceCriteria: Add QuickLogBar component between header and timeline on /diario page
Horizontal scrollable row of favorite cards showing: method icon + product name + dose
Tap on favorite card opens the new entry form pre-filled with favorite data (product, method, dose, symptoms pre-selected), user just confirms time and symptom severity
Calls useLogFromFavorite on submit
If no favorites yet, show subtle hint 'Salve um registro como favorito para acesso rapido'
Manage favorites: small gear/edit icon that shows list with delete option
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Diary summary card on patient dashboard
userStories.description: As a patient, I want to see a diary summary on my main dashboard.
acceptanceCriteria: Add 'Diario de Tratamento' card to /painel (DashboardPage), matching the existing card style (Perfil Clinico, Documentos, Associacoes cards)
Card content: total entries this month, last entry (product name + time + relative time like 'ha 2h'), most tracked symptom, overall improvement indicator (average delta)
Uses useDiarySummary hook with current month date range
Empty state: 'Comece seu diario' text + CTA button linking to /diario
Link 'Ver diario completo' at bottom navigating to /diario
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Insights tab with charts (install recharts, symptom trends, method distribution)
userStories.description: As a patient, I want to see charts and trends of my treatment in an Insights tab.
acceptanceCriteria: Install recharts as dependency in apps/web
Add tab navigation on /diario: 'Timeline' (default) and 'Insights'

Insights tab shows: line chart of average severity before vs after for top 3 symptoms over time (uses useSymptomTrend), bar chart of administration method distribution, card 'Produto mais eficaz' showing product with best average improvement delta, card 'Consistencia' showing days with entries vs total days in period (e.g., '18 de 30 dias')
Period selector: 30d, 60d, 90d
Uses useDiarySummary and useSymptomTrend hooks
Charts use brand-green palette colors
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
